questions.question: What is the default value of object-fit?
questions.explanation: fill is the default value, stretching the content to fill the container (may distort).

questions.question: Which object-fit value ensures the entire image is visible within the container?
questions.explanation: contain scales the image to fit entirely within the container while maintaining aspect ratio.

questions.question: Which object-fit value may crop parts of the image?
questions.explanation: cover scales to fill the container while maintaining aspect ratio, which may crop parts of the image.

questions.question: On which HTML elements does object-fit work?
questions.explanation: object-fit only works on replaced elements like and

questions.question: What does object-position do?
options: Positions the content within its box
questions.explanation: object-position positions the media content within its box, similar to background-position.

questions.question: Which object-fit value is equivalent to not applying object-fit at all?
questions.explanation: none displays the content at its original intrinsic size without any resizing.

questions.question: What does scale-down do?
options: Acts as none or contain, whichever is smaller
questions.explanation: scale-down compares none and contain and uses the smaller result.

questions.question: Why combine aspect-ratio with object-fit: cover?
options: Ensures consistent container sizing before images load
questions.explanation: aspect-ratio gives the container a predictable size before images load, preventing layout shifts.

questions.question: What does object-fit: contain do when the container has a different aspect ratio?
questions.explanation: contain preserves aspect ratio and shows empty space (letterboxing) if the container shape doesn't match.

questions.question: How do you focus on the top portion of an image using object-position?
questions.explanation: object-position: 50% 20% centers horizontally and focuses near the top, ideal for portrait headshots.

questions.question: Which value of object-position is the default?
questions.explanation: The default object-position is 50% 50% (center), which centers the content within its box.

questions.question: Can object-fit be applied to video elements?
options: Yes, object-fit works on all replaced elements including <video>
questions.explanation: object-fit applies to all replaced elements, including <video> and <iframe>.

questions.question: What is the effect of object-fit: fill on an image with a different aspect ratio than its container?
options: Stretches/distorts the image to fill exactly
Shows the image at original size
questions.explanation: fill stretches the image to exactly match the container dimensions, which may distort the aspect ratio.

questions.question: Which scenario is object-fit: cover best suited for?
options: Hero banners and thumbnails where cropping is acceptable
questions.explanation: cover is ideal for hero banners, thumbnails, and avatars where full coverage matters more than showing the entire image.

questions.question: What is the difference between object-fit and background-size?
options: object-fit applies to replaced element content; background-size applies to background images
object-fit is for layout; background-size is for styling
questions.explanation: object-fit controls replaced element content sizing; background-size controls background image sizing.

questions.question: How does object-fit behave on inline images without explicit dimensions?
options: It works but the image uses its natural dimensions as the container
questions.explanation: Without explicit dimensions, the container sizes to the image's natural size, limiting object-fit's visible effect.

questions.question: Which browsers fully support object-fit?
questions.explanation: object-fit is supported in all modern browsers. IE11 needs a polyfill for support.

questions.question: What is the purpose of object-fit in a responsive image grid?
options: Ensures all images have consistent sizing despite different aspect ratios
questions.explanation: object-fit makes images with different aspect ratios display uniformly within equal-sized grid cells.

questions.question: Can object-position use percentage values?
options: Yes, percentages position relative to the container
questions.explanation: object-position accepts percentages, keywords (center, top, etc.), and length values.

questions.question: What happens when object-fit: none is used on a very large image?
options: The image is displayed at original size and may overflow the container
questions.explanation: object-fit: none displays the image at its natural intrinsic size, which may overflow the container.
